items.title: Blueprint directory path mismatch: configs/templates/ vs configs/blueprints/
items.description: The 6 blueprint YAMLs live in configs/templates/ but ARCHITECTURE.md and CLAUDE.md specify configs/blueprints/ as the target path. CQ-009 (blueprint loader) must know which path to use. Must resolve before CQ-009 implementation or the loader will target wrong directory.
items.groomingNotes: BLOCKING: Resolve before CQ-009 sprint starts — the blueprint loader must not guess which directory to read. Decision: rename configs/templates/ to configs/blueprints/ (preferred — aligns with canonical ARCHITECTURE.md spec and CQ-009 implementation target). Also: the _sprint_order note in backlog.json references 'configs/templates/' — update it to 'configs/blueprints/' after rename. Single-step fix. Must be done in the same sprint slot as or immediately before CQ-009.
items.intent: configs/blueprints/ is the canonical path. Rename configs/templates/ before CQ-009 implementation begins. The loader targets one path, not two.

items.title: src/ scaffold has server/ and ui/ dirs not in target architecture
items.description: Empty scaffold dirs src/server/ and src/ui/ exist but are absent from target architecture in CLAUDE.md and ARCHITECTURE.md. Fix: update both docs to include src/server/ (Hono server for CQ-040) and src/ui/ (htmx assets). Doc-only change. Steps: 1. Update CLAUDE.md Key Source Layout — add src/server/ and src/ui/ with their purpose. 2. Update docs/ARCHITECTURE.md package structure to match. 3. Verify no other spec contradicts.
acceptanceCriteria: CLAUDE.md target architecture lists src/web/ with ownership (Hono + htmx, CQ-040)
No scaffold directory in src/ contradicts the spec
CQ-040 has an unambiguous target path in the architecture docs
items.groomingNotes: UPDATED: CQ-040 (web dashboard) is DONE and uses src/web/ — not src/server/ + src/ui/ as the spec assumed. The actual canonical path is src/web/. Fix: update CLAUDE.md Key Source Layout and docs/ARCHITECTURE.md to replace src/server/ + src/ui/ entries with src/web/ (Hono server, htmx assets, 7 pages). Doc-only change, no code moves. Single PR touching two docs files.
items.intent: The target architecture spec must be the single source of truth for src/ layout. No scaffold directories that contradict it.

items.title: Remove extraneous npm dependencies
items.intent: No unused packages in package.json. npm install size is minimal.
items.description: package.json includes several unused packages: @emnapi/core, @emnapi/runtime, @emnapi/wasi-threads, @tybys/wasm-util, tslib. These are not imported anywhere in the codebase and add unnecessary install weight. Steps: (1) verify each package is unused via grep; (2) npm uninstall each confirmed-unused package; (3) run tests to confirm nothing breaks. Low-risk cleanup.
items.groomingNotes: Extraneous packages confirmed by codebase exploration: @emnapi/core, @emnapi/runtime, @emnapi/wasi-threads, @tybys/wasm-util, tslib. Verify none are imported before removing — grep for each package name. Run full test suite after removal. Zero risk if grep confirms they're unused.

items.title: src/secure/ layout doesn't match target architecture
items.intent: CLAUDE.md Key Source Layout accurately describes src/secure/ — no phantom directories, no missing directories. New contributors find security code on first look.
items.description: CLAUDE.md specifies src/secure/harden/, src/secure/firewall/, src/secure/validate/, src/secure/sandbox/ — none of these directories exist. Actual layout: src/secure/sanitizer/ (input injection firewall, not general sandbox), credentials/, audit/, scanner/. The misalignment creates confusion about where to find security logic. Hardening code lives in src/build/docker/posture.ts, landmine validation in src/config/validate.ts. Fix: update CLAUDE.md Key Source Layout to reflect actual directory structure: replace sandbox->sanitizer, add note about validate living in config/, add note that harden is in build/docker/. Clarifies architecture for contributors without requiring code moves.
items.groomingNotes: Doc-only fix. Target CLAUDE.md Key Source Layout: change secure/sandbox/ -> secure/sanitizer/ (input injection firewall). Add note that hardening/posture logic is in build/docker/. Add note that validate (14 landmines) is in config/validate.ts. Also update docs/ARCHITECTURE.md package structure to match. No code moves — just accurate docs. Note: secure/firewall/ WILL exist after CQ-005 completes, so that entry can stay as-is in the docs.

items.title: Firewall allowlist uses hand-rolled YAML parser
items.intent: Firewall allowlist is parsed reliably. No hand-rolled YAML in the codebase.
items.description: src/build/launcher/firewall.ts loadAllowlist() implements a custom line-by-line YAML parser instead of using the 'yaml' package already in package.json. The hand-rolled parser is brittle — it won't handle quoted values, inline lists, comments, or multi-document YAML correctly. Steps: (1) replace loadAllowlist() to use yaml.parse() from the 'yaml' package; (2) update allowlist.yaml type to match parsed structure; (3) add tests covering edge cases (quoted domains, comments, empty file). Scope: small — one function in one file.
items.groomingNotes: SPRINT WITH CQ-005 — this is co-work for the CQ-005 sprint, not a standalone item. The loadAllowlist() function in src/build/launcher/firewall.ts (moving to src/secure/firewall/firewall.ts as part of CQ-005) hand-parses YAML line by line. The yaml package (v2.8.2) is already a project dependency. Fix: import { parse } from 'yaml'; use parse() to load allowlist.yaml; type the result as { domain: string; port?: number; comment?: string }[]. One function change, zero risk. Must be in the same commit as CQ-005 firewall work — do not ship the moved firewall.ts with the hand-rolled parser still in place.

items.title: sanitizer uses process.env.HOME instead of os.homedir()
items.intent: Audit log and quarantine paths are always computed correctly — never fallback to /root. os.homedir() is the single correct API for user home directory.
items.description: src/secure/sanitizer/sanitizer.ts:24-26 computes DEFAULT_OPS_DIR using process.env.HOME ?? '/root' instead of os.homedir() from the 'os' module. The os module is the correct cross-platform API — process.env.HOME is undefined in some container and test environments. If HOME is unset, audit logs and quarantined files are written to /root, which is an incorrect path and may be unwritable. Fix: import { homedir } from 'os'; replace the HOME env var reference with homedir(). The correct pattern is already used in src/config/paths.ts. Steps: (1) open src/secure/sanitizer/sanitizer.ts; (2) add homedir import from 'os'; (3) replace process.env.HOME ?? '/root' with homedir(); (4) run existing sanitizer tests to confirm no regression. Scope: one-line change in one file.
items.groomingNotes: One-line fix in one file. src/secure/sanitizer/sanitizer.ts:25 — replace process.env.HOME ?? '/root' with homedir() from 'os' module. Pattern already used in src/config/paths.ts. No dependencies. Run existing sanitizer tests after change. Zero-risk.

items.title: Gateway port 18789 hardcoded in 23+ locations across 12 files
items.intent: Gateway port has a single source of truth. No literal 18789 appears outside src/config/defaults.ts. Changing the default port requires editing one line.
items.description: The Gateway port 18789 is hardcoded as a literal in at least 23 places across 12 files including src/build/launcher/preflight.ts:20, src/operate/doctor/checks.ts:589, and others. There is no single source of truth for this value. Steps: (1) grep codebase for 18789 to enumerate all instances; (2) define GATEWAY_DEFAULT_PORT = 18789 constant in src/config/defaults.ts (create file if absent) and export it; (3) replace all literal 18789 references with the constant import; (4) run full test suite to confirm no regression. Note: the port is configurable in openclaw.json — this change normalizes the fallback value, not the config surface. Scope: grep + search-replace across ~12 files, no logic change.
items.groomingNotes: grep for 18789 gives all instances. Define GATEWAY_DEFAULT_PORT in src/config/defaults.ts and import everywhere. No logic change — search-replace across ~12 files. Full test suite must pass after. No dependencies on any in-progress work.

items.title: Silent catch blocks in evolve/ swallow errors with no logging
items.intent: No error in the evolve/ module is silently discarded. Every catch block either logs the error or re-throws it. Failures are observable without enabling debug mode.
items.description: 20+ catch blocks across the evolve/ module swallow errors silently, making failures invisible in production and in tests. Key locations: src/evolve/lifecycle/destroy.ts:75,126 (catch { return []; }), src/evolve/memory/preferences.ts:167 (catch swallows preference load failure), src/evolve/skills/stage.ts:174 (catch swallows stage setup failure), src/evolve/migrate/google-parser.ts:172 (catch swallows parse failure), src/evolve/tools/manifest.ts:31 (catch returns empty manifest). Pattern: these are non-fatal paths that return defaults on failure — acceptable, but the error should be logged for observability. Steps: (1) search evolve/ for 'catch {' and 'catch (_' patterns; (2) for each non-fatal catch, add console.warn() or structured log call with the error and context; (3) for catch blocks in test paths, ensure test expectations still pass; (4) do NOT convert non-fatal catches to throws — keep the fallback behaviour, just add logging. Scope: ~20 one-line additions across evolve/.
items.groomingNotes: Sweep evolve/ for 'catch {' and 'catch (_'. Add console.warn(err, context) in each non-fatal catch. Do NOT change fallback return values — keep existing error-handling behaviour, only add observability. Key files: destroy.ts, preferences.ts, stage.ts, google-parser.ts, manifest.ts. Run evolve/ tests after. No dependencies.

items.title: Silent catch blocks in operate/ swallow errors with no logging
items.intent: No error in the operate/ module is silently discarded. Every catch block either logs the error or re-throws it. Diagnostic failures are always observable.
items.description: 20+ catch blocks across the operate/ module swallow errors silently. Key locations: src/operate/doctor/checks.ts (14 instances at lines 84,145,248,257,297,339,378,407,480,496,560,568,595,640), src/operate/monitor/alerts.ts (lines 64,213,224,282,319), src/operate/status/status.ts (lines 131,164,216). Critically, silent catches in doctor/checks.ts mean diagnostic failures are invisible — a check that fails to run is indistinguishable from a check that passes. Steps: (1) search operate/ for 'catch {' and 'catch (_' patterns; (2) for each non-fatal catch, add console.warn() or structured log call with the error and context; (3) do NOT convert non-fatal catches to throws — keep fallback behaviour, just add logging. Scope: ~22 one-line additions across operate/.
items.groomingNotes: PRIORITY NOTE: doctor/checks.ts has 14 silent catches — a check that fails to run is indistinguishable from a check that passes. Doctor is the hero feature. Silent failures here directly undermine the product's core value. Sprint this before BUG-012 and BUG-014. Sweep operate/ for 'catch {' and 'catch (_'. Add console.warn(err, context) in each non-fatal catch. Do NOT change fallback return values — keep existing error-handling behaviour, only add observability. Priority files: doctor/checks.ts (14 instances — MOST IMPORTANT), monitor/alerts.ts (5 instances), status/status.ts (3 instances). Run operate/ tests after. No dependencies.

items.title: Silent catch blocks in build/ swallow errors with no logging
items.intent: No error in the build/ module is silently discarded. Every catch block either logs the error or re-throws it. Preflight, firewall, and service failures must be observable.
items.description: 14+ catch blocks across the build/ module swallow errors silently. Key locations: src/build/launcher/firewall.ts (lines 98,106,127,137,165 — firewall rule failures), src/build/launcher/preflight.ts (lines 93,112,205,219 — pre-flight check failures), src/build/services/add.ts (lines 42,53,114,159,165 — service addition failures), src/build/docker/build.ts (line 166 — Docker build failure). Firewall and preflight silent catches are especially dangerous — a failed preflight that swallows its error allows a broken deploy to proceed silently. Steps: (1) search build/ for 'catch {' and 'catch (_' patterns; (2) for each non-fatal catch, add console.warn() or structured log call with the error and context; (3) do NOT convert non-fatal catches to throws — keep fallback behaviour, just add logging. Scope: ~14 one-line additions across build/.
items.groomingNotes: SEQUENCING: CQ-005 moves src/build/launcher/firewall.ts to src/secure/firewall/firewall.ts. The 5 firewall.ts instances in this bug will be at the wrong path if fixed before CQ-005. DO NOT touch launcher/firewall.ts. FIX NOW (10 instances, safe): launcher/preflight.ts (4 instances — lines 93,112,205,219), services/add.ts (5 instances — lines 42,53,114,159,165), docker/build.ts (1 instance — line 166). AFTER CQ-005: follow-up fix for the 5 firewall instances at src/secure/firewall/firewall.ts. Sprint this item for the 10 safe instances only. Add console.warn(err, context) in each non-fatal catch. Do NOT change fallback return values — keep existing error-handling behaviour, only add observability. Run build/ tests after.

items.title: Silent catch blocks in secure/ swallow errors with no logging
items.intent: No error in the secure/ module is silently discarded. Scanner and audit failures must be logged. A scanner that silently fails is indistinguishable from a clean scan.
items.description: 12+ catch blocks across the secure/ module swallow errors silently. Key locations: src/secure/scanner/git.ts (lines 37,50,69 — git history scan errors), src/secure/scanner/walk.ts (lines 41,62,70 — file walk errors), src/secure/audit/logger.ts (lines 59,88,118,148,184,216 — audit log write failures), src/secure/credentials/probes.ts (line 185 — credential probe failures). The audit/logger.ts instances are particularly critical — if audit log writes fail silently, the tamper-evident audit trail has invisible gaps. Steps: (1) search secure/ for 'catch {' and 'catch (_' patterns; (2) for each non-fatal catch, add console.warn() or structured log call with the error and context; (3) do NOT convert non-fatal catches to throws — keep fallback behaviour, just add logging. Scope: ~12 one-line additions across secure/.
items.groomingNotes: PRIORITY NOTE: audit/logger.ts has 6 silent catches — if audit log writes fail silently, the tamper-evident audit trail has invisible gaps. This directly undermines the transparency promise ('you know what your agent did'). Sprint this before BUG-012. Sweep secure/ for 'catch {' and 'catch (_'. Add console.warn(err, context) in each non-fatal catch. Do NOT change fallback return values — keep existing error-handling behaviour, only add observability. Priority files: audit/logger.ts (6 instances — MOST IMPORTANT, tamper-evident trail gaps), scanner/git.ts (3 instances), scanner/walk.ts (3 instances), credentials/probes.ts (1 instance). Run secure/ tests after. No dependencies.

items.title: config/paths.ts still uses process.env.HOME instead of os.homedir()
items.intent: os.homedir() is the single API for user home directory throughout the codebase. No process.env.HOME usage remains after BUG-010.
items.description: src/config/paths.ts lines 42 and 51 use process.env.HOME ?? homedir() — they correctly import and fall back to homedir(), but still unnecessarily check process.env.HOME first. BUG-010 established that os.homedir() is the correct cross-platform API and fixed sanitizer.ts, but paths.ts was not updated. While the current code is safe (homedir() is the fallback), the inconsistency violates the intent of BUG-010. Steps: (1) open src/config/paths.ts; (2) find lines using process.env.HOME ?? homedir(); (3) replace each with homedir() directly; (4) verify homedir import from 'os' is present; (5) run existing config/paths tests to confirm no regression. Scope: two-line change in one file.
items.groomingNotes: Two-line fix in one file. src/config/paths.ts lines 42 and 51 — replace process.env.HOME ?? homedir() with homedir() directly. homedir import from 'os' should already be present (it's the fallback). Pattern already fixed in sanitizer.ts (BUG-010). No dependencies. Run existing config tests after change. Zero-risk.

items.title: Silent catch blocks in design/ swallow errors with no logging
items.intent: No error in the design/ module is silently discarded. Blueprint load failures and Ollama inference errors must be logged. A blueprint that fails to load silently is indistinguishable from one that doesn't exist.
items.description: 8 catch blocks across design/ swallow errors silently. Key locations: src/design/configure/smart.ts (lines 122,273 — blueprint lookup and JSON parse failures), src/design/configure/ollama.ts (lines 41,62 — Ollama availability check and model listing failures), src/design/blueprints/loader.ts (line 224 — silently skips a blueprint that fails to load, indistinguishable from missing blueprint), src/design/tools/approve-action.ts (lines 50,89,139 — queue JSON parse, Telegram config read, and Telegram API call failures). The loader.ts instance is especially problematic — a blueprint that fails validation or load is silently skipped, the user sees an incomplete blueprint list with no explanation. Steps: (1) search design/ for 'catch {' and 'catch (_' patterns, excluding writer.ts cleanup catches (intentional best-effort); (2) for each non-fatal catch, add console.warn() with the error and context; (3) do NOT convert non-fatal catches to throws — keep fallback behaviour; (4) run design/ tests to confirm no regression. Scope: ~8 one-line additions across 4 files.
items.groomingNotes: 8 silent catches in 4 files. Most critical: loader.ts:224 silently skips failed blueprint loads — the user sees an incomplete list with no explanation. approve-action.ts:50,89,139 swallows queue and Telegram errors. smart.ts:122,273 swallows inference fallback errors. ollama.ts:41,62 swallows availability and model list errors. Exclude writer.ts cleanup catches (intentional try/catch in error path — best-effort cleanup is correct). Add console.warn(err, context) to each non-fatal catch. Do NOT change fallback return values. Run design/ tests after. No dependencies on in-progress work. Pattern identical to BUG-012 through BUG-015.

items.title: Silent catch blocks in cloud/ swallow errors with no logging
items.intent: No error in the cloud/ module is silently discarded. Heartbeat reporter failures must be logged — a silent heartbeat calculation error produces incorrect health metrics with no trace.
items.description: 2 catch blocks in src/cloud/heartbeat/reporter.ts swallow errors silently. Line 85: directory size calculation error swallowed, returns partial result (wrong size metrics). Line 101: credentials file read error swallowed, returns 0 count (wrong credential count). Both produce incorrect health data sent to the remote monitoring layer with no indication they failed. Steps: (1) open src/cloud/heartbeat/reporter.ts; (2) find catch blocks at lines 85 and 101; (3) add console.warn() with the error and context for each; (4) do NOT change fallback return values — keep returning partial/0 values on failure; (5) run cloud/ tests to confirm no regression. Scope: 2 one-line additions in one file.
items.groomingNotes: 2 silent catches in one file. heartbeat/reporter.ts:85 (directory size calc error → wrong size reported to remote), heartbeat/reporter.ts:101 (credentials file read error → 0 reported instead of actual count). Both produce incorrect health telemetry silently. Add console.warn(err, context) to each. Keep fallback return values unchanged. Run cloud/ tests after. No dependencies.

items.title: Silent catch block in gateway/health.ts hides connection errors
items.intent: Gateway health check failures must log the underlying error. When the agent goes down, the operator needs the actual error (connection refused, timeout, auth failure) — not just 'down'.
items.description: src/gateway/health.ts line 104 has a catch block that swallows the error and returns status 'down' with no logging. While the 'down' fallback is correct, the actual error (connection refused vs. TLS error vs. auth failure) is completely invisible to the operator. This makes diagnosing gateway connectivity failures much harder. Note: gateway/client.ts:215 has an intentional 'Ignore non-JSON messages' catch — do NOT touch that one. Steps: (1) open src/gateway/health.ts; (2) find catch block at line 104; (3) add console.warn() logging the error before returning the 'down' status; (4) do NOT change the 'down' return value; (5) run gateway/ tests to confirm no regression. Scope: 1 one-line addition in one file.
items.groomingNotes: 1 silent catch in health.ts:104. Returns 'down' correctly but swallows the actual error (connection refused? timeout? auth?). Operator can't diagnose why health check failed. Add console.warn(err) before the 'down' return. Do NOT touch client.ts:215 ('Ignore non-JSON messages' — intentional). Do NOT change return value. Run gateway/ tests after. No dependencies.

items.title: Post-CQ-005: silent catch blocks in new src/secure/firewall/firewall.ts
items.intent: The new firewall module created by CQ-005 must not have silent catch blocks. Firewall rule failures that are silently swallowed allow a misconfigured firewall to go undetected.
items.description: BUG-014 intentionally deferred the 5 silent catch blocks in src/build/launcher/firewall.ts because CQ-005 moves that code to src/secure/firewall/firewall.ts. When CQ-005 is complete, the moved firewall code must be checked for silent catches and fixed. The original 5 locations were lines 98,106,127,137,165 in the old firewall.ts — these handle iptables rule application, removal, and verification errors. Steps: (1) CQ-005 must be complete first; (2) open src/secure/firewall/firewall.ts (the new CQ-005 file); (3) search for 'catch {' and 'catch (_' patterns; (4) add console.warn() with error and context for each non-fatal catch; (5) do NOT convert non-fatal catches to throws — keep fallback behaviour; (6) run firewall tests (using EXEC_DRY=1) after. Scope: ~5 one-line additions in one file. Blocked by CQ-005.
items.groomingNotes: BLOCKED BY CQ-005. Do not sprint until CQ-005 completes — the file doesn't exist yet. After CQ-005: src/secure/firewall/firewall.ts will contain the moved firewall code. The original 5 silent catches were at lines 98,106,127,137,165 of src/build/launcher/firewall.ts. Find the moved equivalents in the new file and add console.warn(err, context) to each. All tests must use EXEC_DRY=1 (no real iptables calls in CI). BUG-014 grooming notes documented this follow-up explicitly. Mark readyForSprint after CQ-005 sprint completes.

items.title: Silent catch block in cli/index.ts swallows notification channel config error
items.intent: No error in cli/index.ts is silently discarded. The notification channel config catch must log when it fails so operators know monitoring won't send alerts.
items.description: src/cli/index.ts line 3167 has a catch block with a comment 'No channels configured — monitor still runs, just no notifications' but no logging. If the notification channel config throws due to misconfiguration, the operator has no trace. The monitor continues silently degraded. This is the only silent catch in the cli/ module — the sweep that covered evolve/, operate/, build/, secure/, design/, cloud/, and gateway/ (BUG-012 through BUG-019) did not cover cli/. Steps: (1) open src/cli/index.ts; (2) find catch block at line 3167; (3) add console.warn() logging the error and context before continuing; (4) do NOT change the fallback behavior — monitor should still run without notifications; (5) run cli tests to confirm no regression. Scope: 1 one-line addition in one file.
items.groomingNotes: 1 silent catch in cli/index.ts:3167. Notification channel config fails silently — monitor runs degraded with no alerts sent and no trace. Add console.warn(error, 'notification channel config failed — monitoring will run without alerts') before continuing. Keep fallback behavior unchanged. No dependencies. Pairs with BUG-012 through BUG-019 sweep pattern. Zero-risk one-liner.

items.title: Silent catch block audit for src/config/ module
items.intent: No error in src/config/ is silently discarded. Config parse, schema validation, and file read failures must be observable without enabling debug mode.
items.description: The systematic silent-catch sweep (BUG-012 through BUG-019, BUG-021) covered evolve/, operate/, build/, secure/, design/, cloud/, gateway/, and cli/. The src/config/ module was not included. BUG-016 fixed a specific process.env.HOME issue in config/paths.ts but was not a general silent-catch sweep. Config module files to audit: defaults.ts, paths.ts, validate.ts, loader.ts, types.ts — any silent catches here would hide config parse failures, schema validation errors, or file read problems that are critical to every command. Steps: (1) search src/config/ for 'catch {' and 'catch (_' patterns; (2) for each non-fatal catch, add console.warn() with error and context; (3) do NOT convert non-fatal catches to throws — keep fallback behaviour; (4) run config/ tests to confirm no regression. Scope: likely 2-5 additions, low risk.
items.groomingNotes: No hard dependencies — all modules it references (config/) are fully implemented. Pattern is identical to BUG-012 through BUG-019: search 'catch {' and 'catch (_', add console.warn(err, context) to each non-fatal catch, keep fallback return values unchanged, run tests. Config module is small (defaults.ts, paths.ts, validate.ts, loader.ts, types.ts) so scope is minimal. BUG-016 fixed a specific HOME env issue in paths.ts but was not a silent-catch sweep. This completes the module coverage gap. Zero risk.

items.title: Post-CQ-005: Update CLAUDE.md cross-module security code section
items.intent: CLAUDE.md accurately reflects the canonical firewall location after CQ-005. Contributors find firewall code on first look.
items.description: CQ-005 moves applyFirewall()/removeFirewall() from src/build/launcher/firewall.ts to src/secure/firewall/firewall.ts. After CQ-005 completes, the CLAUDE.md 'Cross-module security code' section still references the old path: 'Egress firewall (iptables CLAWHQ_FWD) → src/build/launcher/firewall.ts'. This will mislead contributors looking for firewall code. Doc-only change, single section in one file. Steps: (1) Confirm CQ-005 is complete and src/secure/firewall/firewall.ts exists; (2) Open CLAUDE.md, find 'Cross-module security code' section; (3) Update firewall entry from src/build/launcher/firewall.ts to src/secure/firewall/firewall.ts; (4) Verify no other CLAUDE.md references point to the old location; (5) No code changes required.
items.groomingNotes: BLOCKED BY CQ-005. Do not sprint until CQ-005 is complete and src/secure/firewall/firewall.ts exists. After CQ-005: single CLAUDE.md edit in the 'Cross-module security code' section — change 'src/build/launcher/firewall.ts' to 'src/secure/firewall/firewall.ts'. Check for any other references to the old path. Doc-only, zero risk, pairs with BUG-020 and CQ-041 in the post-CQ-005 batch. Mark readyForSprint after CQ-005 sprint completes.

items.title: Web dashboard port 3737 hardcoded in 2 locations
items.intent: Dashboard default port has a single source of truth. No literal 3737 appears outside src/config/defaults.ts. Changing the default requires editing one line.
items.description: The dashboard default port 3737 is hardcoded in two locations: src/web/start.ts:10 (const DEFAULT_PORT = 3737) and src/cli/index.ts:3180 (string default "3737" in .option()). This is the same BUG-011 pattern as the Gateway port 18789. If the port is changed in start.ts, the CLI help text stays stale. Steps: (1) add DASHBOARD_DEFAULT_PORT = 3737 to src/config/defaults.ts; (2) import and use it in src/web/start.ts replacing DEFAULT_PORT; (3) import and use it in src/cli/index.ts replacing "3737" string; (4) run tests to confirm no regression. Scope: two files, ~4 lines changed.
items.groomingNotes: Two-location fix in two files. src/web/start.ts:10 (DEFAULT_PORT = 3737) and src/cli/index.ts:3180 (string '3737' in .option()). Add DASHBOARD_DEFAULT_PORT = 3737 to src/config/defaults.ts. Import and replace in both files. Run tests after — zero logic change, pure constant extraction. Pattern identical to BUG-011. No dependencies.

items.title: Ollama default URL hardcoded and inconsistent across 8+ files
items.intent: Ollama default URL has a single source of truth. No literal Ollama URL appears outside src/config/defaults.ts. All runtime references use the same host string.
items.description: The Ollama default URL is hardcoded across 8+ files with inconsistent values — some use http://127.0.0.1:11434 and others use http://localhost:11434, which are technically equivalent but create noise and risk drift. Locations: src/design/configure/ollama.ts:8 (127.0.0.1, named local const DEFAULT_OLLAMA_URL), src/evolve/memory/summarize.ts:54 (127.0.0.1), src/evolve/migrate/extract.ts:75 (127.0.0.1), src/evolve/provider/registry.ts:35 (localhost), src/evolve/integrate/validate.ts:94 (localhost), src/evolve/integrate/registry.ts:78 (localhost, string default). Same pattern as BUG-011 (Gateway port scattered across 12 files). Steps: (1) add OLLAMA_DEFAULT_URL = 'http://127.0.0.1:11434' to src/config/defaults.ts (use 127.0.0.1 to match the design/ module which is the primary Ollama caller); (2) replace all runtime string literals with the constant import; (3) update doc comments to match; (4) run tests. Scope: ~6 file edits, no logic change.
items.groomingNotes: 8+ files with hardcoded Ollama URL. Runtime instances to replace (not doc comments): src/design/configure/ollama.ts:8 (DEFAULT_OLLAMA_URL), src/evolve/memory/summarize.ts:54, src/evolve/migrate/extract.ts:75, src/evolve/provider/registry.ts:35, src/evolve/integrate/validate.ts:94, src/evolve/integrate/registry.ts:78 (defaultValue field). Use 127.0.0.1 (not localhost) as canonical value — matches the design/ module which is the primary Ollama caller; 127.0.0.1 is also safer in containerized environments. Add OLLAMA_DEFAULT_URL = 'http://127.0.0.1:11434' to defaults.ts. Doc comments (types.ts, migrate/types.ts, design/configure/smart.ts) update to match. Run tests — no logic change. No dependencies.

items.title: WhatsApp Graph API version mismatch: v17.0 in validate vs v21.0 in connect
items.intent: WhatsApp integration uses a single, consistent Graph API version throughout. No version mismatch between the validation probe and the connect flow.
items.description: Two files reference different versions of the WhatsApp/Facebook Graph API: src/build/launcher/connect.ts:23 uses v21.0 (const WHATSAPP_API = 'https://graph.facebook.com/v21.0'), while src/evolve/integrate/validate.ts:115 uses v17.0. The credential validation probe and the actual connection flow must use the same API version. A probe that passes on v17.0 endpoints may not guarantee v21.0 compatibility and vice versa. Steps: (1) decide canonical version (v21.0 is newer — prefer that); (2) add WHATSAPP_API_VERSION = 'v21.0' and WHATSAPP_API_BASE = 'https://graph.facebook.com' to src/config/defaults.ts; (3) update connect.ts to use the constant; (4) update integrate/validate.ts to use the same version; (5) run tests. Scope: 3 files, ~5 line change.
items.groomingNotes: Version mismatch: connect.ts uses v21.0, integrate/validate.ts uses v17.0. Canonical version: v21.0 (newer). Add WHATSAPP_GRAPH_API_VERSION = 'v21.0' to src/config/defaults.ts (do NOT add full URL — just the version segment to keep it composable). Update connect.ts WHATSAPP_API const to template with the version constant. Update integrate/validate.ts:115 to use the same version. Check evolve/integrate/registry.ts for any hardcoded version in egressDomains or config (just 'graph.facebook.com' — no version there, fine). Run tests. No dependencies — standalone fix.

items.title: JSON parse missing try-catch in cloud/ state loaders
items.intent: No corrupted cloud state file causes an unhandled exception. All cloud state loaders recover gracefully with logged warnings and safe fallbacks.
items.description: Four cloud module state loaders use bare JSON.parse without try-catch: src/cloud/fleet/discovery.ts:43 (readFleetRegistry), src/cloud/trust-modes/switch.ts:44 (readTrustModeState), src/cloud/commands/queue.ts:46 (readQueueState), src/cloud/heartbeat/reporter.ts:39 (loadHeartbeatState). If any of these JSON files is corrupted (partial write, disk error, manual edit), the function throws an unhandled exception that propagates through the entire command, crashing with no useful error message. The same pattern was fixed in evolve/tools/manifest.ts and evolve/approval/queue.ts (BUG-012 sweep) but cloud/ was missed. Steps: (1) wrap JSON.parse in try-catch with console.warn + fallback return in each of the 4 functions; (2) fallback values: fleet={version:1,agents:[]}, trust-mode={version:1,mode:'paranoid',connected:false,changedAt:...}, queue={version:1,pending:[],history:[]}, heartbeat=null-or-default-state; (3) run cloud/ tests to confirm no regression. Scope: 4 one-liner additions across 4 files.
items.groomingNotes: 4 cloud/ state loaders missing try-catch: cloud/fleet/discovery.ts:43 (readFleetRegistry), cloud/trust-modes/switch.ts:44 (readTrustModeState), cloud/commands/queue.ts:46 (readQueueState), cloud/heartbeat/reporter.ts:39. Fallback values: fleet={version:1,agents:[]}, trust-mode={version:1,mode:'paranoid',connected:false,changedAt:new Date().toISOString()}, queue={version:1,pending:[],history:[]}, heartbeat=return null or log only. Pattern to follow: evolve/tools/manifest.ts:28-34 (try { return JSON.parse(raw); } catch (err) { console.warn('[cloud] ...', err); return fallback; }). Run cloud/ tests after each change. All 4 files are standalone — no blocking dependencies. Same pattern as BUG-012 through BUG-019 sweep but for cloud/ module.

items.title: JSON parse missing try-catch in evolve/ manifest loaders
items.intent: No corrupted manifest file in evolve/ causes an unhandled exception. All manifest loaders recover gracefully with logged warnings and safe defaults.
items.description: Six evolve module manifest loaders use bare JSON.parse without try-catch: src/evolve/role/manifest.ts:54 (loadRoleManifest), src/evolve/provider/manifest.ts:26 (loadProviderManifest), src/evolve/integrate/manifest.ts:26 (loadIntegrationManifest), src/evolve/memory/lifecycle.ts:61, src/evolve/rollback/capability-snapshot.ts:67, src/evolve/skills/rollback.ts:39. These files exist and can be corrupted (disk error, partial write, manual edit). The same pattern was correctly handled in evolve/tools/manifest.ts and evolve/skills/lifecycle.ts (both have try-catch + console.warn). Steps: (1) apply the same try-catch pattern to the 6 missing loaders; (2) fallback: role manifest seeds built-in roles only, others return empty version-1 state; (3) add console.warn(err, context) in each catch; (4) run evolve/ tests to confirm no regression. Scope: 6 try-catch wrappers across 6 files. Pattern is identical to BUG-012/BUG-015 sweep.
items.groomingNotes: 6 evolve/ manifest loaders missing try-catch: evolve/role/manifest.ts:54, evolve/provider/manifest.ts:26, evolve/integrate/manifest.ts:26, evolve/memory/lifecycle.ts:61, evolve/rollback/capability-snapshot.ts:67, evolve/skills/rollback.ts:39. Correct pattern already in evolve/tools/manifest.ts:28-34 and evolve/skills/lifecycle.ts:38-45 — copy this pattern to the 6 missing files. Fallbacks: role={version:1,roles:[...BUILTIN_ROLES],assignments:{}}, provider={version:1,providers:[]}, integrate={version:1,integrations:[]}, memory lifecycle=return null or empty, rollback snapshots=[]. Add console.warn('[evolve] Failed to read <manifest>:', err) in each catch. No dependencies. Run evolve/ tests after. Scope: 6 files, each ~5 lines added.

items.title: Dockerfile Stage 2 binary URL and path not validated before template injection
items.intent: Generated Dockerfiles never contain malformed or shell-unsafe content from binary tool config. URL scheme must be https:// and destPath must be a safe absolute path.
items.description: src/build/docker/dockerfile.ts:81-82 interpolates binary.url and binary.destPath directly into Dockerfile content via template strings: 'RUN curl -fsSL "${binary.url}" -o "${binary.destPath}"'. There is no validation that: (1) binary.url is a valid https:// URL before insertion, (2) binary.destPath is a safe absolute path with no newlines, quotes, or shell metacharacters. A malformed URL causes a confusing Docker build failure. A path with embedded newlines could inject additional Dockerfile instructions. The Stage2Config type accepts any string values. Steps: (1) add validateBinaryUrl(url: string): void helper that throws if url is not a valid https:// URL (use new URL(url) + check protocol); (2) add validateBinaryDestPath(path: string): void that throws if path contains newlines, quotes, or backslashes; (3) call both before inserting into Dockerfile lines; (4) call validation in the Stage 2 generator and/or in the tool manifest layer where binaries are added; (5) run docker/ tests to confirm no regression. Scope: ~15 lines in one file.
items.groomingNotes: SECURITY UPGRADE — elevated from 'should' to 'must'. This is an active Dockerfile injection vulnerability: destPath with embedded newlines can inject arbitrary Dockerfile instructions into the Stage 2 build. CQ-031 (tool install) is already shipped so the attack surface is live. For a platform whose core promise is 'security by default', shipping a Dockerfile injection vector is directly contradictory. The fix is ~15 lines and low-risk — no reason to defer. Grooming notes unchanged: validateBinaryUrl() + validateBinaryDestPath() in dockerfile.ts:81-82, called before template insertion. No dependencies. Run docker/ tests after.

items.title: Hardcoded API probe URLs in evolve/integrate/validate.ts
items.intent: Credential probe URLs have a single source of truth in config/defaults.ts. No literal API base URLs appear in validate.ts.
items.description: src/evolve/integrate/validate.ts hardcodes three API probe endpoints as string literals: line 51 'https://api.anthropic.com/v1/models', line 66 'https://api.openai.com/v1/models', line 80 'https://api.telegram.org/bot{token}/getMe' (template). These are the same pattern as BUG-025 (Ollama URL) and BUG-026 (WhatsApp Graph API) — probe endpoints hardcoded in one module with no single source of truth. Steps: (1) add ANTHROPIC_API_BASE='https://api.anthropic.com', OPENAI_API_BASE='https://api.openai.com', TELEGRAM_API_BASE='https://api.telegram.org' to src/config/defaults.ts; (2) import and replace the three string literals in validate.ts; (3) run evolve/ tests to confirm no regression. Scope: 4 files, ~6 lines changed. Same pattern as BUG-025/BUG-026.
items.groomingNotes: Pattern identical to BUG-025 (Ollama URL) and BUG-026 (WhatsApp API). Three API base URLs in evolve/integrate/validate.ts:51,66,80. Add ANTHROPIC_API_BASE, OPENAI_API_BASE, TELEGRAM_API_BASE to src/config/defaults.ts (base URLs only, not paths). Import in validate.ts. Verify no other files also hardcode these bases (check evolve/credentials/probes.ts — those probe endpoints may already derive from these). Run evolve/ tests after. No dependencies — standalone fix.

items.title: File permission mode literals scattered across 3+ files
items.intent: File permission modes have a single source of truth in config/defaults.ts. No bare octal literals appear outside that file.
items.description: The octal literal 0o600 appears as a magic number in src/design/configure/writer.ts:60 (mode: 0o644 default parameter), src/build/launcher/preflight.ts:161 (mode !== 0o600), src/operate/doctor/checks.ts:159 (mode !== 0o600). The constants MODE_0600 and MODE_0644 are already defined locally in src/secure/credentials/credential-store.ts and env-store.ts — but those definitions are not exported or reused. Same pattern as BUG-011 (Gateway port) and BUG-025 (Ollama URL). Steps: (1) add FILE_MODE_SECRET = 0o600, FILE_MODE_CONFIG = 0o644, FILE_MODE_EXEC = 0o755 constants to src/config/defaults.ts; (2) replace magic octal literals in writer.ts, preflight.ts, doctor/checks.ts with imports; (3) remove the duplicate local MODE_0600 constants from credential-store.ts and env-store.ts, import from defaults.ts instead; (4) run full test suite. Scope: ~5 files, no logic change.
items.groomingNotes: Pattern identical to BUG-011 (Gateway port) and BUG-024 (Dashboard port). Octal literals 0o600, 0o644 scattered across writer.ts, preflight.ts, doctor/checks.ts. Consolidation target: src/config/defaults.ts. Export FILE_MODE_SECRET=0o600, FILE_MODE_CONFIG=0o644. Remove duplicate local MODE_0600 from credential-store.ts and env-store.ts — import from defaults.ts instead. No logic change — pure constant extraction. Run full test suite after (these mode values affect file creation so tests cover the behaviour). No blocking dependencies.

items.title: DEFAULT_REPO_URL hardcoded in build/installer/source.ts
items.intent: OpenClaw repository URL has a single source of truth in config/defaults.ts. No hardcoded repo URL appears in source.ts.
items.description: src/build/installer/source.ts:23 defines const DEFAULT_REPO_URL = 'https://github.com/nicepkg/openclaw.git' as a local constant that is never exported or centralised. This is the canonical OpenClaw repository URL — if the repo URL ever changes (mirror, org rename), there is no single place to update it. Same BUG-011/BUG-025 pattern as other scattered constants. Steps: (1) add OPENCLAW_REPO_URL = 'https://github.com/nicepkg/openclaw.git' to src/config/defaults.ts; (2) import and replace DEFAULT_REPO_URL in source.ts; (3) check for any other hardcoded references to this URL in the codebase (grep for 'nicepkg/openclaw'); (4) run build/ tests. Scope: 2 files, ~3 lines changed.
items.groomingNotes: Single local constant in one file. source.ts:23 defines DEFAULT_REPO_URL = 'https://github.com/nicepkg/openclaw.git'. Add OPENCLAW_REPO_URL to defaults.ts, import and replace. Also grep for 'nicepkg/openclaw' across the whole codebase to catch any other occurrences (installer docs, README mentions are fine — only fix runtime string literals in src/). Run build/ tests after. Zero-risk constant extraction. No blocking dependencies.

items.title: Ollama default model name 'llama3:8b' hardcoded across 7 locations
items.intent: Ollama default model has a single source of truth in config/defaults.ts. No literal model name string appears in runtime code outside that file.
items.description: The Ollama default model 'llama3:8b' is hardcoded in 7 locations across 5 files: src/design/configure/ollama.ts:10 (const DEFAULT_MODEL = 'llama3:8b'), src/design/configure/smart.ts:51 (const DEFAULT_LOCAL_MODEL = 'llama3:8b'), src/design/configure/wizard.ts:61 (const DEFAULT_LOCAL_MODEL = 'llama3:8b'), src/web/server.tsx:238 (fallback string literal), src/evolve/migrate/extract.ts:77 (?? fallback), src/evolve/memory/summarize.ts:56 (?? fallback). Same pattern as BUG-025 (Ollama URL) and BUG-026 (WhatsApp API version). Steps: (1) add OLLAMA_DEFAULT_MODEL = 'llama3:8b' to src/config/defaults.ts; (2) import and replace runtime literals in ollama.ts:10, smart.ts:51, wizard.ts:61, server.tsx:238, extract.ts:77, summarize.ts:56; (3) web/pages/init.tsx:59 HTML placeholder text can stay as-is (presentational); (4) run design/, evolve/, and web/ tests to confirm no regression. Scope: 6 files, ~8 lines changed. Zero logic change — pure constant extraction.
items.groomingNotes: Pattern identical to BUG-025 (Ollama URL) and BUG-031 (file mode constants). 7 hardcoded 'llama3:8b' literals across 5 runtime files: design/configure/ollama.ts:10, design/configure/smart.ts:51, design/configure/wizard.ts:61, web/server.tsx:238, evolve/migrate/extract.ts:77, evolve/memory/summarize.ts:56. Add OLLAMA_DEFAULT_MODEL = 'llama3:8b' to defaults.ts. Import and replace in all 6 runtime files. web/pages/init.tsx HTML placeholder text (presentational) does not need updating. Run tests for design/, evolve/, web/ after. No blocking dependencies — standalone constant extraction.

items.title: Cloud state writers missing try-catch on atomic file writes
items.intent: Trust mode and command queue writes must never fail silently. A trust mode switch to paranoid that fails silently leaves the agent in a less-restrictive security posture with no indication — this is a security failure, not a reliability failure.
items.description: Four cloud module state writers perform atomic writes (writeFileSync + renameSync) without try-catch: src/cloud/heartbeat/reporter.ts:56-61 (heartbeat state), src/cloud/trust-modes/switch.ts:70-75 (trust mode state), src/cloud/commands/queue.ts:63-68 (command queue), src/cloud/fleet/discovery.ts:60-65 (fleet registry). BUG-027 fixed the read side (JSON.parse) but missed the write side. A disk write failure silently discards the update — a trust mode switch to 'paranoid' could fail with no warning, leaving the agent in an unexpected state. Steps: (1) src/cloud/trust-modes/switch.ts:70-75 — wrap in try-catch and THROW on error (critical path — caller must know if trust mode change failed); (2) src/cloud/commands/queue.ts:63-68 — wrap in try-catch and throw (enqueue failure must propagate); (3) src/cloud/heartbeat/reporter.ts:56-61 — wrap in try-catch and console.warn (best-effort, non-critical); (4) src/cloud/fleet/discovery.ts:60-65 — wrap in try-catch and console.warn (best-effort); (5) run cloud/ tests after each change. Scope: 4 files, ~8 additions. Note: trust-modes/switch.ts and commands/queue.ts must throw; reporter.ts and discovery.ts log-only.
items.groomingNotes: PRIORITY UPGRADED: should → must. Same classification as BUG-029 (Dockerfile injection) and BUG-035 (approval queue silent drop) — all three are cases where a silent failure directly violates a core security promise. For this item: trust-modes/switch.ts:70-75 and commands/queue.ts:63-68 MUST THROW (security-critical path — caller must know the state change failed); heartbeat/reporter.ts:56-61 and fleet/discovery.ts:60-65 log-only (best-effort, non-critical). BUG-027 fixed the read side (JSON.parse); this is the write-side counterpart. Run cloud/ tests after each file. No blocking dependencies.

items.title: approve-action tool saveQueue() writes approval queue without error handling
items.intent: Approval queue writes never fail silently. A disk write error in saveQueue() must be caught and logged — a silently dropped write means an approval is lost, directly violating the no-auto-send guarantee.
items.description: src/design/tools/approve-action.ts:59 defines a saveQueue() function that calls writeFileSync(QUEUE_FILE, JSON.stringify(queue, null, 2)) without try-catch. This is an agent-side script installed into the deployed agent's tools directory. If the file write fails (disk full, permission error), the approval is silently dropped — the user receives a Telegram approval notification but their approve/reject action is never persisted. The no-auto-send guarantee depends on this write succeeding. Note: this is a GENERATED file. The fix must be applied in the template/generator that produces it, not in the output file directly. Steps: (1) locate the generator for approve-action.ts in src/design/tools/ (likely tools.ts or approve-action generator); (2) update the saveQueue() template to wrap writeFileSync in try-catch with console.error(err) and process.exit(1) on failure (the tool is a CLI script — failure must be loud); (3) verify the fix propagates to newly generated tools by running design/tools/ tests; (4) if existing deployed approve-action.ts files are not regenerated automatically, note in the fix that re-running 'clawhq init' or 'clawhq build' regenerates the tool. Scope: 1 generator file, ~5 lines added to template.
items.groomingNotes: PRIORITY UPGRADED: 'should' → 'must'. The no-auto-send guarantee is a core product promise — it appears in marketing, README, and product docs. A silently dropped saveQueue() write means the user receives a Telegram approval notification but their approve/reject action is lost and never persisted. The agent then proceeds (or doesn't) with no record of what the user chose. This is a direct violation of the safety contract, not a quality-of-life fix. The fix is in the generator that produces approve-action.ts — wrap writeFileSync in try-catch with console.error + process.exit(1) (loud failure is correct for a CLI tool). Existing deployed agents get the fix on next 'clawhq build' Stage 2 rebuild. No blocking dependencies — run design/tools/ tests to verify generated output. Sprint alongside CQ-005 or immediately after.

items.title: Path traversal risk in web dashboard /api/init deployDir input
items.intent: deployDir submitted via web form must be sanitized and resolved to prevent writing files outside the user's home directory. No path traversal from web init.
items.description: src/web/server.tsx:240-245 accepts a user-supplied deployDir via POST /api/init and resolves ~ with a simple string replace, but does not validate the resulting path with path.resolve() or check it stays within the user's home directory. A crafted deployDir value like '../../sensitive' could cause agent config files to be written outside ~/.clawhq/. The web dashboard runs on localhost:3737 so this is local-only, but it is still a path traversal vulnerability. Steps: (1) import { resolve } from 'path' in server.tsx; (2) after resolving ~, apply path.resolve() to canonicalize; (3) validate the resolved path starts with homedir() — if not, reject with 400 error; (4) run web/ tests to confirm no regression. Scope: ~5 lines in one file.
items.groomingNotes: Security fix in web/server.tsx:240-245. The deployDir input from POST /api/init is resolved via simple ~ replacement but not canonicalized. Fix: (1) after rawDeployDir.replace('~', homedir()), call path.resolve() on the result; (2) check resolvedDeployDir.startsWith(homedir()) — if false, return c.html(<InitResult success={false} message='Invalid deployment directory'/>) with status 400; (3) require 'path' is already imported (check — if not, add it). The gatewayPort input on line 239 also lacks range validation: add isNaN check (line 254 already does this — pattern already there). Focus on path traversal fix only. Scope: 5 lines max. Run web/ tests after. No blocking dependencies.

items.title: parseInt/parseFloat NaN propagation in CLI commands
items.intent: All numeric CLI options must reject non-numeric input with a clear error message. NaN must never propagate silently into cron schedules, interval timers, or config fields.
items.description: Multiple CLI option parsers use parseInt/parseFloat without guarding against NaN. Key cases: (1) src/cli/index.ts:1920 — digestHour: Math.max(0, Math.min(23, parseInt(opts.digestHour, 10))) — Math.max(0, NaN) = NaN, so a non-numeric --digest-hour writes NaN into the cron schedule; (2) src/cli/index.ts:1959 — memoryLifecycleInterval: Math.max(1, parseFloat(opts.memoryLifecycleInterval)) * 3600_000 — same issue; (3) src/cli/index.ts:2705 — maxEgressDomains: parseInt(opts.maxEgress, 10) — no NaN check, written directly to config; (4) src/cli/index.ts:1845 — lineCount: parseInt(opts.lines, 10) — no NaN guard before use. Pattern: the dashboard port on line 3183-3184 already validates correctly (isNaN check + clear error). Extend that pattern to the 4 affected options. Steps: (1) digestHour — validate isNaN before clamping, exit with error if NaN; (2) memoryLifecycleInterval — same; (3) maxEgressDomains — add isNaN guard; (4) lineCount — add isNaN guard. Scope: ~8 additions across one file. Pattern to follow: lines 3183-3184 in same file.
items.groomingNotes: 4 specific locations in src/cli/index.ts. Pattern to follow: lines 3183-3184 (isNaN check + process.exit). Fix each: (1) line 1920: after parseInt, add if (isNaN(digestHour)) { console.error('--digest-hour must be a number 0-23'); process.exit(1); } — move parseInt to its own line first; (2) line 1959: after parseFloat, add NaN guard; (3) line 2705: after parseInt, add NaN guard before assigning to maxEgressDomains; (4) line 1845: add NaN guard on lineCount before passing to logs command. Also note: lines 1583, 1919 already use Math.max() guards that coerce NaN to the minimum (Math.max(1, NaN) = NaN actually — check these too). Run cli/ tests after. No blocking dependencies. Scope: one file, ~12 lines.

items.title: Gateway and operate/ timeout constants scattered — not in config/defaults.ts
items.intent: All timeout and interval constants in gateway/ and operate/ must have a single source of truth in config/defaults.ts. Changing timeouts must require editing one file.
items.description: The same BUG-011/BUG-025 constant-scattering pattern applies to timeout and interval values. Key locations: src/gateway/client.ts:31 (DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS = 10_000), src/gateway/client.ts:32 (CONNECT_TIMEOUT_MS = 5_000), src/gateway/health.ts:19 (DEFAULT_INTERVAL_MS = 30_000), src/gateway/health.ts:20 (DEFAULT_HEALTH_TIMEOUT_MS = 5_000), src/operate/doctor/checks.ts:26 (EXEC_TIMEOUT_MS = 15_000), src/secure/credentials/probes.ts:37 (timeoutMs = 10_000). These are local-only constants — if you need to tune the gateway timeout for a slow network, you must find all the right files. The credentials probe timeout is especially important as it affects CI/CD pipelines. Steps: (1) add GATEWAY_RPC_TIMEOUT_MS = 10_000, GATEWAY_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_MS = 5_000, GATEWAY_HEALTH_INTERVAL_MS = 30_000, GATEWAY_HEALTH_TIMEOUT_MS = 5_000, DOCTOR_EXEC_TIMEOUT_MS = 15_000, CREDENTIALS_PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS = 10_000 to src/config/defaults.ts; (2) import and replace local constants in each file; (3) run gateway/ and operate/ tests. Scope: 6 files, no logic change. Pattern identical to BUG-011.
items.groomingNotes: 6 local timeout constants to centralize. Files: gateway/client.ts (DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS, CONNECT_TIMEOUT_MS), gateway/health.ts (DEFAULT_INTERVAL_MS, DEFAULT_HEALTH_TIMEOUT_MS), operate/doctor/checks.ts (EXEC_TIMEOUT_MS), secure/credentials/probes.ts (timeoutMs default). Add 6 named constants to defaults.ts, import and replace each. Keep the same variable names locally if needed for backwards compat, or replace inline. Also check operate/monitor/recovery.ts:21, operate/monitor/alerts.ts:27, operate/status/status.ts:26-27, operate/logs/logs.ts:17, operate/updater/updater.ts:27 — all define local EXEC_TIMEOUT_MS = 30_000; each can import the same DOCTOR_EXEC_TIMEOUT_MS or a new OPERATE_EXEC_TIMEOUT_MS depending on value. SCOPE LIMIT: Only the 6 gateway/ and operate/doctor/ + secure/credentials/ instances — do not sweep all operate/ in one PR. Defer the remaining operate/ monitor/recovery/alerts/status/logs/updater to a follow-up. Run gateway/ and operate/ tests after. No blocking dependencies.
